title: Microglossa cabulica
taxonomy: Microglossa cabulica belongs to the family Campanulaceae. This family is commonly known as the bellflower family. The genus Microglossa is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical Africa. Microglossa cabulica is one of the species within the genus and is characterized by its unique bell-shaped flowers. The classification of Microglossa cabulica is further supported by its morphological features and genetic analysis.

intro: Microglossa cabulica, also known as Afghan root, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of fever and pain. The bioactive constituents of Microglossa cabulica include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Microglossa cabulica is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or a powder to treat a range of health conditions.
